import torch
import torch.nn as nn

class ResBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, hidden, dropout=0.0):
        super().__init__()
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(hidden, hidden)
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(hidden, hidden)
        self.act = nn.GELU()
        self.norm = nn.LayerNorm(hidden)
        self.drop = nn.Dropout(dropout) if dropout > 0 else nn.Identity()
    def forward(self, x):
        h = self.fc2(self.drop(self.act(self.fc1(self.norm(x)))))
        return x + h

class ResMLP(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_dim, out_dim, hidden=1024, depth=8, dropout=0.0):
        super().__init__()
        self.inp = nn.Linear(in_dim, hidden)
        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList([ResBlock(hidden, dropout) for _ in range(depth)])
        self.norm = nn.LayerNorm(hidden)
        self.out = nn.Linear(hidden, out_dim)
    def forward(self, x):
        h = self.inp(x)
        for b in self.blocks:
            h = b(h)
        return self.out(self.norm(h))

# Hybrid: vertical conv + MLP head
class HybridModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, hidden=512, n_conv=4, kernel=5, mlp_hidden=1536, mlp_depth=6, dropout=0.0):
        super().__init__()
        # Inputs: 60 t, 60 q, 4 scalars (ps, solin, lhflx, shflx)
        self.in_channels = 2 + 4  # t, q, plus scalars broadcast
        self.conv_in = nn.Conv1d(self.in_channels, hidden, kernel, padding=kernel//2)
        self.convs = nn.ModuleList()
        for _ in range(n_conv):
            self.convs.append(nn.Sequential(
                nn.Conv1d(hidden, hidden, kernel, padding=kernel//2),
                nn.GELU(),
                nn.Conv1d(hidden, hidden, kernel, padding=kernel//2),
            ))
        self.act = nn.GELU()
        # After conv: hidden x 60 -> flatten + scalars
        flat_dim = hidden * 60
        # Use 1x1 conv head to predict per-level outputs (t_tend, q_tend)
        self.level_head = nn.Conv1d(hidden, 2, 1)
        # Scalar head from pooled features
        pooled = hidden * 2  # mean+max
        self.scalar_mlp = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(pooled + 4, mlp_hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Linear(mlp_hidden, mlp_hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Linear(mlp_hidden, 8),
        )
    def forward(self, x):
        # x: [B, 124] = [t(60), q(60), ps, solin, lhflx, shflx]
        B = x.shape[0]
        t = x[:, :60]
        q = x[:, 60:120]
        scal = x[:, 120:124]  # [B,4]
        # Build [B, 6, 60]
        scal_b = scal.unsqueeze(-1).expand(-1, -1, 60)
        feat = torch.stack([t, q], dim=1)  # [B,2,60]
        feat = torch.cat([feat, scal_b], dim=1)  # [B,6,60]
        h = self.act(self.conv_in(feat))
        for c in self.convs:
            h = h + c(h)
        # Per-level outputs
        lvl = self.level_head(h)  # [B,2,60]
        ptend_t = lvl[:, 0, :]
        ptend_q = lvl[:, 1, :]
        # Pooled
        mean_p = h.mean(-1)
        max_p = h.max(-1).values
        pooled = torch.cat([mean_p, max_p, scal], dim=1)
        scalar_out = self.scalar_mlp(pooled)  # [B,8]
        out = torch.cat([ptend_t, ptend_q, scalar_out], dim=1)  # [B,128]
        return out

class VerticalTransformer(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_model=256, nhead=8, nlayers=6, dim_ff=1024):
        super().__init__()
        self.embed = nn.Linear(6, d_model)
        self.pos = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(1, 60, d_model))
        layer = nn.TransformerEncoderLayer(d_model, nhead, dim_ff, batch_first=True, activation='gelu', norm_first=True)
        self.enc = nn.TransformerEncoder(layer, nlayers)
        self.level_head = nn.Linear(d_model, 2)
        self.scalar_head = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(d_model*2 + 4, 512), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(512, 8))
    def forward(self, x):
        t = x[:, :60]
        q = x[:, 60:120]
        scal = x[:, 120:124]
        scal_b = scal.unsqueeze(1).expand(-1, 60, -1)
        feat = torch.stack([t, q], dim=-1)
        feat = torch.cat([feat, scal_b], dim=-1)  # [B,60,6]
        h = self.embed(feat) + self.pos
        h = self.enc(h)
        lvl = self.level_head(h)
        ptend_t = lvl[:, :, 0]
        ptend_q = lvl[:, :, 1]
        pooled = torch.cat([h.mean(1), h.max(1).values, scal], dim=-1)
        scalar_out = self.scalar_head(pooled)
        return torch.cat([ptend_t, ptend_q, scalar_out], dim=1)
